item.title: Phase0-tree-sitter: Integrate tree-sitter-markdown into em-core
item.intent: em-core can parse any markdown string into a typed AST using tree-sitter-markdown (split_parser branch). The parse result is a Rust struct tree, not a raw tree-sitter CST. Every downstream feature (formatting, doctor, WYSIWYM decorations) reads from this AST — no component parses markdown ad-hoc.
item.description: Integrate tree-sitter-markdown (the split_parser branch, which separates block and inline grammars) into em-core as the single parser. Port the AST node types to Rust enums that map to the tree-sitter node kinds. The AST types must cover: headings, paragraphs, code blocks (fenced and indented), lists and list items, blockquotes, tables (GFM), links, images, emphasis, strong, inline code, thematic breaks, and frontmatter (YAML block detection). Provide a parse(text: &str) -> SyntaxTree function in em-core. Do NOT use the String-based Document from M0 for the AST — the parser operates on a &str input and returns an owned tree. Reference for algorithm inspiration (not code): the legacy Swift files in reference/ (TreeSitterConversion.swift and the EMFormatter/EMDoctor rule files). Note that reference/ does not contain an EMParser directory — use TreeSitterConversion.swift as the tree-sitter node-type mapping reference.
steps: Add tree-sitter and tree-sitter-markdown as Cargo dependencies (using the split_parser branch via git dependency)
Create em-core/src/ast.rs defining Rust enums for all major node kinds listed in the description
Create em-core/src/parser.rs implementing parse(text: &str) -> SyntaxTree that walks the tree-sitter CST
Map tree-sitter node type strings (e.g. 'atx_heading', 'fenced_code_block') to the Rust AST enums
Handle GFM extensions: table, strikethrough, task list item, autolink
Handle YAML frontmatter: detect a leading '---' block and wrap it in a FrontmatterNode
Write unit tests in em-core/src/parser.rs that parse representative markdown fragments and assert on the resulting AST structure
Run cargo test -p em-core and confirm all new parser tests pass

acceptanceCriteria.text: cargo test -p em-core --test parser passes with parser tests covering major node kinds
tree-sitter-markdown is a real dependency of em-core (confirmed by cargo tree)
parse() returns a typed AST (not a raw tree-sitter cursor) — proven by tests that assert on Rust enum variants
cargo test --workspace passes with no failures
